# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
  - name: my-app-storage-bucket
    type: gcp-types/storage-v1:buckets
    properties:
      # Bucket names must be globally unique. Replace 'your-project-id' to ensure uniqueness.
      # You can also use $(project) to include your project ID. E.g., $(project)-my-app-bucket
      name: your-project-id-my-app-bucket 
      project: $(project)
      location: us-central1 # Choose an appropriate region
      storageClass: STANDARD
      labels:
        environment: mvp
        app: my-app

  - name: my-app-sql-instance
    type: gcp-types/sqladmin-v1beta4:instances
    properties:
      name: my-app-sql-instance
      databaseVersion: POSTGRES_14 # Or MYSQL_8_0, etc.
      region: us-central1 # Must match the region for the instance
      settings:
        tier: db-f1-micro # Smallest tier for MVP
        backupConfiguration:
          enabled: true
          startTime: '03:00' # Example backup start time
        ipConfiguration:
          ipv4Enabled: true
          requireSsl: false # For initial MVP simplicity; strongly recommend 'true' for production
          # authorizedNetworks: # Uncomment and configure for specific IP access, e.g., your office IP
          #   - value: "0.0.0.0/0" # WARNING: Allows access from any IP. Restrict in production.
        storageAutoResize: true
        storageAutoResizeLimit: 0 # Unlimited auto-resize
        dataDiskSizeGb: 20 # Initial disk size
      labels:
        environment: mvp
        app: my-app

  - name: my-app-instance-template
    type: gcp-types/compute-v1:instanceTemplates
    properties:
      name: my-app-instance-template
      properties:
        machineType: e2-micro # Smallest machine type for MVP
        disks:
          - deviceName: boot
            type: PERSISTENT
            boot: true
            autoDelete: true
            initializeParams:
              sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-11
        networkInterfaces:
          - network: global/networks/default # Using default VPC network
            accessConfigs:
              - name: External NAT
                type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT # Required for external internet access (e.g., to fetch packages)
        serviceAccounts:
          - email: $(projectNumber)-compute@developer.gserviceaccount.com # Default Compute Engine SA
            scopes:
              - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform # Broad scope for MVP; narrow down in production
        metadata:
          items:
            - key: startup-script
              value: |
                #!/bin/bash
                sudo apt-get update
                sudo apt-get install -y nginx
                echo "Hello from GCP Deployment Manager on $(hostname)! This is the MVP." | sudo tee /var/www/html/index.nginx-debian.html
                sudo systemctl restart nginx
        labels:
          environment: mvp
          app: my-app

  - name: my-app-health-check
    type: gcp-types/compute-v1:httpHealthChecks
    properties:
      name: my-app-health-check
      port: 80
      requestPath: / # Path to check for health status
      checkIntervalSec: 5
      timeoutSec: 5
      unhealthyThreshold: 2
      healthyThreshold: 2
      description: Basic HTTP health check for web servers

  - name: my-app-mig
    type: gcp-types/compute-v1:instanceGroupManagers
    properties:
      name: my-app-mig
      zone: us-central1-a # Choose a specific zone for the Managed Instance Group
      baseInstanceName: my-app-instance # Prefix for instance names
      instanceTemplate: $(ref.my-app-instance-template.selfLink)
      targetSize: 1 # Start with 1 instance for MVP
      autoHealingPolicies: # Configure auto-healing based on the health check
        - healthCheck: $(ref.my-app-health-check.selfLink)
          initialDelaySec: 300 # Wait before starting auto-healing checks
      labels:
        environment: mvp
        app: my-app

  - name: my-app-backend-service
    type: gcp-types/compute-v1:backendServices
    properties:
      name: my-app-backend-service
      protocol: HTTP
      portName: http # Name associated with the port in the instance template's firewall rule
      timeoutSec: 30
      healthChecks:
        - $(ref.my-app-health-check.selfLink)
      backends:
        - group: $(ref.my-app-mig.selfLink) # Link to the Managed Instance Group
      labels:
        environment: mvp
        app: my-app

  - name: my-app-url-map
    type: gcp-types/compute-v1:urlMaps
    properties:
      name: my-app-url-map
      defaultService: $(ref.my-app-backend-service.selfLink) # Direct all traffic to the backend service
      labels:
        environment: mvp
        app: my-app

  - name: my-app-http-proxy
    type: gcp-types/compute-v1:targetHttpProxies
    properties:
      name: my-app-http-proxy
      urlMap: $(ref.my-app-url-map.selfLink) # Link to the URL map
      description: Target HTTP proxy for the load balancer
      labels:
        environment: mvp
        app: my-app

  - name: my-app-forwarding-rule
    type: gcp-types/compute-v1:globalForwardingRules
    properties:
      name: my-app-forwarding-rule
      portRange: 80 # HTTP port
      target: $(ref.my-app-http-proxy.selfLink) # Direct traffic to the HTTP proxy
      description: Global forwarding rule for HTTP load balancer
      labels:
        environment: mvp
        app: my-app

outputs:
  - name: storageBucketName
    value: $(ref.my-app-storage-bucket.name)
  - name: sqlInstanceConnectionName
    value: $(ref.my-app-sql-instance.connectionName)
  - name: loadBalancerIpAddress
    value: $(ref.my-app-forwarding-rule.IPAddress)
